inspection: Check starter battery terminal voltage using a multimeter.
Inspect terminal connectors for security and integrity.
repair: Loosen the negative (-) terminal connector clamp and slide it off.
Loosen the positive (+) terminal connector clamp and slide it off.
Remove battery strap or brackets, extract battery from casing.
Slide in the new fully-charged lead-acid AGM battery.
Connect and torque the positive (+) terminal first, then the negative (-) terminal.

checklist: Terminal leads are clamped tightly with zero play.

rollback: Disconnect terminals starting with negative first.
Re-seat previous battery or check master fuse for blown state.

inspection: Inspect battery terminal leads for green/white sulfate powder crust.
Verify if connection wiggle cuts electrical display console power.
repair: Disconnect negative terminal connector clamp, then positive connector clamp.
Scrub terminals and connectors using wire brush and baking soda water mixture.
Dry leads thoroughly and apply thin film of dielectric grease.
Reconnect positive terminal lead first, then negative terminal lead securely.

checklist: Terminal posts are clean with zero powdery oxidation.

rollback: Check connection bolt threads for damage.

inspection: Unscrew spark plug using 16mm socket wrench and inspect tip color.
repair: Scrub electrode tip using fine wire brush or replacement plug.
Verify plug gap thickness matches spec (e.g. 0.7mm) using feeler gauge.
Screw in plug by hand clockwise to prevent thread cross damage.
Tighten to specification using plug torque wrench, reconnect boot.

checklist: Plug electrode is clean with dry metallic finish.

rollback: Remove plug and re-check gap spacing.
Ensure plug boot is pushed down until clicks.

inspection: Measure mid-run vertical chain movement slack using ruler.
repair: Loosen main rear axle wheel spindle nut.
Rotate chain adjuster bolts on both sides equally to tighten slack.
Ensure swingarm alignment index lines match exactly on both sides.
Torque rear axle nut to 80 Nm specification.
Spray high-speed dry lube along chain run.

checklist: Wheel alignment index lines match perfectly.

rollback: Back out adjuster bolts to return to previous state.

inspection: Pull brake lever and verify spongy feel with near handlebar contact.
Inspect lines and seals for hydraulic fluid leaks.
repair: Remove master cylinder reservoir cap.
Attach clear tube to caliper bleed valve and submerge end in brake fluid container.
Pump lever 3-4 times, hold it down, and crack open bleed valve.
Close bleed valve before releasing the lever.
Repeat until no bubbles exit tube, topping up reservoir as needed.

checklist: Brake lever feels firm and rigid.

rollback: Top up reservoir and bleed again.
Ensure reservoir cap seal is not crimped.

inspection: Rotate wheel slowly and check tread surface for nail or puncture entry.
Inspect side walls for cracks or deformation.
repair: Remove nail/debris using pliers.
Clean puncture channel using reamer file tool.
Prep rubber plug with vulcanizing glue cement, insert using needle tool.
Cut plug excess tail flush with tread surface.
Re-inflate tire to recommended 32 PSI pressure.

checklist: Soapy water spray shows zero air bubbles at plug site.
Tire pressure remains stable at 32 PSI.

rollback: Re-inflate or prepare for internal tube repair.
